content.verdict: Viceroy Bali is the most glamorous boutique resort in Ubud — 25 private pool villas perched dramatically above the Petanu River Valley, with panoramic rice terrace views that stop you in your tracks every morning. The combination of intimate scale, the renowned Apéritif restaurant (one of Asia's finest), the rooftop NIGHT bar, and impeccable villa service makes this a favourite for style-conscious honeymooners who want the best of Ubud without compromise.
content.best_room: Every villa at Viceroy Bali has its own private pool and sweeping valley views — the distinction is in scale. The Pool Villa is the most popular for honeymooners: intimate, beautifully furnished with hand-carved Balinese hardwoods, and positioned at the valley's edge. The River Valley Suite adds a living room and larger terrace for couples who want room to breathe.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Valley Views
itinerary_7_nights.description: Arrive from Denpasar Airport (approximately 90 minutes' drive) and check into your private pool villa. The views of the Petanu Valley rice terraces from your terrace are immediately arresting — layers of emerald green cascading down to the river far below. Order a welcome cocktail from your villa host and watch the afternoon light transform the valley as the sun drops.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Apéritif Dinner Experience
itinerary_7_nights.description: Devote the morning to your private pool and a lazy villa breakfast. In the evening, make your reservation at Apéritif restaurant non-negotiable — this is one of the most celebrated dining experiences in Asia, serving a theatrical multi-course menu of Indonesian and global flavours in a stunning Dutch colonial dining room. The cocktail bar before dinner is equally memorable.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Ubud Spa & Art
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning spa session at Viceroy's Lembah Spa — the signature Balinese massage using warm coconut oil is deeply therapeutic. Afternoon exploring Ubud's world-renowned art district: the Neka Art Museum, the Agung Rai Museum of Art, and the artisan workshops of Mas village are all within easy reach. Return for sunset cocktails at the NIGHT rooftop bar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Rice Terrace Walk
itinerary_7_nights.description: Join the guided morning walk through the Campuhan Ridge and down through the Petanu Valley rice terraces — the path takes about two hours and ends in a small village where locals are tending the paddies. A private car meets you at the village for the return. Afternoon is free for villa time, a swim, and reading on your terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Temple & Ceremony
itinerary_7_nights.description: Visit Pura Tirta Empul, Bali's most sacred water temple, for a morning blessing ceremony — a profoundly moving experience. Continue to Tegallalang Rice Terraces for the most photogenic views in Bali. Evening back at Viceroy for a private villa dinner arranged by your host: a Balinese feast served on your terrace under the stars.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cooking & Yoga
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning yoga on the sunrise deck, then a private Balinese cooking class in the Viceroy kitchen — learning to prepare the complex spice pastes (base gede and base megenep) that underpin all Balinese cooking. Eat what you create for lunch, then devote the rest of the day to your pool and a second spa treatment.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: One final sunrise over the Petanu Valley from your villa terrace — the morning mist in the valley and the sound of the gamelan from the village below creates a farewell that is hard to leave. Breakfast in bed, a last swim, and then the drive back to Denpasar. Viceroy Bali is the kind of place that makes return visits almost inevitable.

caveats: Viceroy is inland — Ubud has no beach. For couples wanting both jungle and beach, plan Viceroy as one leg of a Bali split stay.
The drive from the airport takes 90 minutes and can reach two hours in peak Bali traffic — plan accordingly.
Only 25 villas means the resort books up months in advance during peak season (June–August, December–January).

faqs.question: Is Viceroy Bali good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Viceroy Bali is one of Ubud's finest honeymoon resorts — every villa has a private pool and rice terrace views, the Apéritif restaurant is one of Asia's best, and the boutique scale ensures personalised, attentive service throughout. Honeymooners seeking glamour and intimacy in the Balinese highlands will not be disappointed.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: May through September is Ubud's dry season — clear mornings, bright sunshine, and the rice terraces at their most verdant. December through February sees the terraces flooded for planting, which is extraordinarily beautiful but can bring afternoon showers.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Reserve your Apéritif dinner immediately after booking your stay — it fills up weeks ahead. Lembah Spa treatments should also be pre-booked. Alert the resort to your honeymoon on reservation for a complimentary welcome experience.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, Viceroy Bali is not adults-only, but the resort's boutique nature and overall sophisticated atmosphere means it skews strongly towards adult couples and honeymooners.

faqs.question: What's the best room for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: All 25 villas have private pools and valley views, making any choice a good one. The Viceroy Pool Villa is the most popular for honeymooners — intimate, beautifully designed, and perfectly positioned at the valley's edge. Upgrade to the River Valley Suite for more space.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS) in Denpasar and take a private car or hotel transfer to Ubud — approximately 90 minutes. The Viceroy can arrange airport transfers at additional cost.
